headline: Experts on NDTV Profit discussed HDFC Bank, HAL, Bharti Airtel, ITC, and Mazagon Dock, offering buy, hold, and avoid recommendations. HAL and HDFC Bank were strong buys; ITC was flagged for risks. Bharti Airtel and Mazagon Dock were hold recommendations with growth potential.
HDFC Bank dropped 2%, weighing on the market sentiment. Indigo rose 2% on crude price gains, while ONGC fell 4% post weak Q4.
RBI may review HDFC Bank's differential interest rates; MSRDC case probe concluded. HDFC Bank denies wrongdoing; RBI response to the matter is still pending.
HDFC Bank contributes to market pressure as Nifty nears 24,000.
HDFC Bank allegedly disguised crores as marketing spend to provide higher interest to a state firm.
HDFC Bank shares fell 2% following a vigilance probe on alleged irregular payments. HDFC Bank's 20.8% YTD decline impacts indices, funds, and investor confidence.

HDFC Bank fell 2.26% to Rs 761.25 after an internal probe into Rs 45-cr interest payments to MSRDC. The probe coincided with ex-Chairman Chakraborty's resignation, raising governance concerns and market scrutiny.
HDFC Bank sees 22 lakh shares traded through a block deal.
HDFC Bank drops 2% as Sensex opens choppy; Nifty above 23,900. Eternal gains 1% amid market's mixed opening performance.
Indian indices closed flat as HDFC Bank's 2.6% drop offset metal stocks' 1.7% rally. Hindalco and Nalco rose 4.2% and 4.1%, while FPIs offloaded Indian shares worth $24.3B YTD.

MSCI adjustments impact stocks today; Bajaj Finance, HUL, TCS, Infosys, and others face weightage declines with outflows up to $204M. Federal Bank, MCX, NALCO, and Indian Bank join MSCI Standard Index; Adani Power, BPCL, Nykaa, and Trent see increased weightage.
TCS partners with AI leader Mistral to integrate Mistral Forge globally. TCS to build Centre of Excellence for AI, focusing on BFSI, manufacturing, healthcare, public sectors.
TCS secures multi-year deal with Sweden's SKF for IT landscape modernization using AI. TCS shares fell 0.2% to Rs 2,271.75 on BSE, underperforming Sensex's 0.1% dip.

SKF awarded TCS a global AI-led business transformation contract to modernize IT and enhance operational efficiency. TCS will leverage AI to build a resilient, scalable digital foundation for SKF, driving growth and customer value globally.
Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) launches SovereignSecure Cloud in Europe.
Sensex trades flat, Nifty around 24,000; Infosys, TCS lead gains.
30 Nifty stocks lose Rs 22.6 lakh crore in 2026; HDFC Bank, TCS lead.
TCS, Tata Consumer Products, and three other stocks go ex-date today. Key updates include a Rs 98 dividend payout and a 6:1 bonus issue.
Stocks like TCS, IRB Infra, Hatsun Agro, and GPT Infra trade ex-dividend on May 26, 2026. Hatsun Agro offers ₹10/share, LTM ₹53/share, and TCS ₹31/share as interim dividends.
RIL seeks shareholder nod for ₹16.64T transactions over five years. The transactions involve Jio Platforms and Reliance Jio Infocomm as per AGM notice.
Reliance Industries integrates AI for growth in streaming and digital content. Focus on boosting media, entertainment, and viewer engagement per PTI report.
Reliance Industries unveils FY26 masterplan with Jio 2.0, retail expansion, and AI initiatives.
RIL invested over ₹41,000 Cr in FY26 for clean energy, FMCG, and digital. The funds supported subsidiaries and strategic arms in scaling renewables, FMCG, and tech.
Reliance's luxury retail arm reports reduced losses in operations. Designer labels under the unit are underperforming, impacting growth.
Reliance to operationalise 40 GWh BESS by H2 2026, scalable to 100 GWh. RIL's clean energy projects include HJT modules, CBG expansion, and Rs 75,000 cr investment.
Reliance Industries predicts near-term demand sensitivity to macro conditions. Reliance Retail focuses on expansion, innovation, and risk management to strengthen its ecosystem.

RIL's FY27 outlook cites vulnerability to geopolitical risks, sluggish oil demand, and market volatility amid Middle East conflict. RIL progresses on new energy with BESS giga factory nearing 40 GWh capacity and plans for green hydrogen by 2032.
Reliance Retail, JioMart, and Campa aim to reshape India's consumption story. Reliance focuses on building affordable Indian brands with global quality.
Reliance Consumer Products aims for multifold FMCG revenue growth by 2030. RIL's FY26 report outlines plans to be a leading branded consumer products firm.
Maruti Suzuki and VinFast grew EV market share to 10.5% in April, up from 3.1% in January. Tata Motors' and MG Motor's combined EV share fell to 58.1%, while Maruti's e Vitara gained traction.
Maruti Suzuki adopts work-from-home policies and limits foreign travel amid West Asia war concerns. The measures align with PM Modi's austerity call to address long-term impacts of the conflict.
Maruti Suzuki announces vehicle recall over suspected defect.
Maruti Suzuki recalls 26 Super Carry vehicles over driver seat defect, ensuring free replacements. Maruti to hike prices by up to Rs 30,000 from June 2026 due to rising input costs and inflation.
Morgan Stanley predicts 37% upside for Maruti Suzuki post price hike. Margins expected to bottom out in Q1 FY27, boosting stock potential.
Sensex trades above 75,300 and Nifty nears 23,700, showing market strength. Maruti Suzuki and ICICI Bank emerge as key contributors to the rally.
Morgan Stanley retains Overweight on Maruti Suzuki with a target price of 17,895.

GIFT Nifty signals a strong start; key stocks include Paytm, Maruti Suzuki, Nykaa, and GAIL due to earnings, deals, and actions. Paytm block deal set at Rs 1,120.65/share; Maruti announces up to Rs 30,000 price hike. Nykaa posts 286% profit growth.
Maruti Suzuki plans to hike prices across models starting next month.
ITC, HUL, Nestle India among 5 FMCG stocks with key dividend payout dates. Investors should track these payout dates for potential dividend opportunities.

FMCG firms brace for slower volume growth in FY27 due to inflation and rising input costs. Companies like Hindustan Unilever, Britannia, and Dabur focus on price hikes and shrinkflation to protect margins.

Sensex fell 135 pts, Nifty flat; Bajaj Finance, Hindustan Unilever, Tech Mahindra among top losers. Apollo Hospitals, Honeywell Automation gained; oil stocks rose on Brent crude's drop below $105/barrel.

Rural-linked firms saw strong Q4FY26 recovery, led by low inflation, infra jobs, and govt welfare boosts. HUL, Britannia, Dabur raised prices to counter rising costs; El Niño risks and crude prices may hurt margins.
FMCG majors like Marico, Dabur, Godrej, and HUL cut ad spends amid demand pressures. Sequential ad spend declines: Marico 5%, Dabur 10%, Godrej 10%, HUL 0.8%.

HUL raises prices for Wheel Detergent, Vim Dishwash Bar, and Ponds Cream in May to counter input cost inflation. Price hikes range from 3.7% to 11.1%, impacting margins amid higher crude, logistics, and packaging costs.
FMCG stocks like Marico, Dabur, Nestle show recovery, Nifty FMCG index rises 7% in a month. Dabur, HUL announce price hikes; valuations of stocks like ITC, Emami near decade-low levels.
Goldman Sachs names 12 'Alpha Picks' including HUL and Paytm as foreign selling slows in Indian markets.
Defence Ministry releases RFP for 5th Gen AMCA, affecting L&T. Other impacted stocks include BEL, Bharat Forge, and BEML.
L&T Mindtree leases 6 lakh sq ft office space in Chennai for ₹4.09 crore/month. The five-year lease is in Larsen & Toubro's IT park, per Propstack data.

L&T's LTEPS partners with EVR Motors to develop advanced EV traction motors for India. The motors will be manufactured in Coimbatore, catering to multiple vehicle segments with high efficiency and localisation.
Larsen & Toubro GeoStructure wins Rs 1,000-2,500 crore contracts from JSW, IWAI, and Mumbai Port.
L&T's GeoStructure unit secures an order worth ₹1,000-₹2,500 crore.

L&T GeoStructure secures its largest piling order from JSW Utkal Steel for a 10 MTPA steel plant in Odisha. Additional orders include inland ship repair facilities in Bihar, UP, and India's first yacht marina in Mumbai.
L&T's valuation shifts from attractive to fair amid robust metrics and strong performance. P/E and P/BV ratios align with historical averages, prompting recalibrated investor outlooks.
LTIMindtree eyes global growth post Randstad deal; Europe revenue to exceed $1B. Focus on AI, GCC expansions; deal EPS neutral, stock closed at ₹4,008.2 on NSE.

L&T Mindtree (LTM) acquires Randstad's Europe, Australia units for $186M, boosting presence in aerospace, BFS, and AI-driven services. The deal raises LTM's European market share from 15% to 20% and aligns with its $10B revenue target by FY31 amid sector-wide acquisition trends.

Brokerages highlight Hyundai, Pine Labs, ONGC, IndusInd Bank, and Sun Pharma with diverse ratings and growth outlooks. Hyundai targets EV growth; Sun Pharma shows strong drug prospects; ONGC faces mixed results; real estate developers gain market share.
Sun Pharma's Unloxcyt shows 50% response rate in skin cancer study. Updated CK 301 101 trial data to be presented at ASCO, highlighting safety and durability.
Sun Pharma seeks reclassification of shares from Promoter Group to Public. Application submitted to exchanges on 26 May 2026 under SEBI Listing Regulations.
Sun Pharma forms higher highs/lows, enters accumulation zone, trades above 50 & 200 EMA. Rising EMAs and increased delivery percentages indicate positive momentum for Sun Pharma.
Sun Pharmaceutical Industries focuses on expanding its specialty portfolio despite global challenges.
Sun Pharma's Q4FY26 performance prompts 3 brokerages to recommend a 'Buy'. However, one brokerage steps back, showing divergent market views.
Brokerages predict up to 25% upside in Sun Pharma, Zydus Lifesciences, and Aurobindo Pharma. Recommendations include BUY, SELL, or HOLD; check target prices for investment decisions.
Top stock recommendations for May 25 include LG Electronics, Eicher Motors, Sun Pharma, NTPC, and GAIL.

Adani Group nears Rs 20 lakh crore market cap, adding Rs 5 lakh crore YTD in 2026. Key players: Adani Power, Ports, Enterprises. Adani Power ranks 11th in India's top 20 by market cap. Growth driven by earnings, green energy focus, and investor sentiment.

BSE Power index surged 24% in 2 months; stocks like BHEL, CG Power, Adani Power hit all-time highs. Hitachi Energy, Adani Green, CG Power report robust growth, benefiting from renewable energy and infrastructure expansion.

PL Capital favors Adani Ports, Adani Power, and Adani Enterprises for strong growth potential in India's infrastructure and energy sectors. Adani Ports leads in logistics with 26% of India's seaborne trade, while Adani Power targets 42 GW capacity by FY32, and AEL drives growth in diversified infrastructure.

5 Adani stocks, including Adani Power, hit 52-week highs; Adani Power surpasses Infosys in market cap at ₹4.77T. Adani Enterprises plans value unlocking via demergers; SBI MF acquired 0.5% stake worth ₹1,435 crore recently.
Adani Power surged 6.8% to Rs 234.15 on May 25, 2026, hitting a 52-week high. The stock marked a fresh all-time peak, continuing its strong momentum after recent declines.

Capital Group increased stakes in Adani Ports (₹74.9B), Adani Power, and Adani Green, while reducing Reliance holdings. Adani stocks surged (Adani Power +94%, Adani Green +35%, Adani Ports +25%) as investor confidence rebounded post-regulatory scrutiny.

Bernstein bullish on Adani Group citing strong execution, financial health, and competitive edge over govt-run entities. Adani Ports (TP: Rs 1,880) and Adani Power (TP: Rs 177) rated Outperform; cautious on Ambuja Cements (TP: Rs 542).
SC declines to interfere with clearances for Adani's MP coal project in Singrauli. The court cited delays in challenging environmental clearances granted earlier.
Adani seals Rs 5,693-cr Jaypee buyout; Adani Power acquires 24% in JPVL. Adani Ports also completes acquisition of JFIL in the Jaypee deal.
JSW Steel sees a descending triangle breakout with close above resistance. Rising volumes, higher highs, and bullish candlesticks signal strong momentum.
JSW Steel touched an all-time high of Rs 1,326.2 on May 27, 2026. The stock reflects strong performance across financial and technical metrics in ferrous metals.
JSW Energy announces ₹4,000 cr QIP and ₹3,150 cr stake monetisation in JSW Steel. Sharad Mahendra highlights dual benefits and objectives of these financial moves.
JSW Steel to start work on ₹1 trillion steel plant on ex-POSCO land. The plant will be located near Paradip, Odisha, after POSCO's failed venture.

Experts suggest holding Apollo Tyres, Siemens; buying JSW Steel, Hero MotoCorp, NMDC. Vodafone Idea calls for cautious profit booking. Challenges include crude price impact on Apollo Tyres, debt issues for Vodafone Idea. JSW Steel, NMDC show upside potential; Siemens sees strength.
JSW Energy sold 25,000,000 JSW Steel shares (1.02% equity) via bulk deal on May 18, 2026. Post-sale, JSW Energy's stake in JSW Steel decreased to 44.29% from 45.32%.

JSW Steel's P/E at 41.9 exceeds industry's 26.4; strong institutional interest supports its Nifty 50 role. Despite Mojo Grade downgrade to Hold, JSW Steel retains sector leadership in India's ferrous metals.
JSW Energy's Rs 3,150-crore JSW Steel stake sale aims to reduce debt. Jefferies discusses implications of the stake sale for JSW Energy's finances.
JSW Energy Ltd sold 25M shares of JSW Steel Ltd at ₹1260 avg price.
Bharti Airtel saw a block deal of 1.37 crore shares, 0.18% equity. Shares traded at Rs 1,832 each, totaling Rs 2,517 crore.
Bharti Airtel and Reliance Jio support premium 5G services for high-paying users. Vodafone Idea opposes, warning against a two-tier internet ecosystem.
India's prepaid and postpaid ARPUs are converging after 5 years, say analysts. Operators' tariff repair efforts have driven this ARPU convergence phase.
Key stocks in focus today include ONGC, Coal India, Bharti Airtel, and IRCTC. KEC International is also among the stocks to watch on May 27.
TRAI report shows Jio nearing Airtel's pace of net additions in Apr'26. Jio's growth in net additions reflects increasing competition with Airtel.

India added 6.18M mobile subscribers in April, down from 8.43M in March; Airtel gained 3.14M users, Jio 2.94M. TRAI released a consultation paper on V2X communication, favoring 4G/5G networks for connected mobility; comments invited by May 2026.
India's telecom subscriber growth slowed in April as major operators saw declines. Key players affected include Bharti Airtel, Jio, BSNL, and Vodafone Idea per TRAI data.
Sensex falls over 150 points; Nifty slips below 24,000. Airtel and IndiGo shares decline by up to 4% during trading.
